- Backlog Item ID: BI-260014
- Title: Formalize information architecture baseline
- Persona/Actor: Data manager and domain operator
- Value statement: For workspace users and implementation teams, establish a clear information architecture so routing, module boundaries, and data ownership remain consistent through MVP delivery.
- Context: Information-architecture decision phase for F1-F6.
- Problem statement: Without explicit F-section decisions, feature growth can produce route sprawl, module coupling, and unclear content ownership.
- Expected outcome: Team can implement one coherent application shell with stable feature boundaries and extension seams.
- Success metric(s):
  - F1-F6 are accepted and encoded in formal artifacts.
  - FR/CR requirements capture shell boundaries, module ownership, and extension constraints.
- Acceptance criteria (behavior):
  - ADR-260017 is created and accepted.
  - REQ-FR-260013, REQ-FR-260014, and REQ-CR-260006 are created and linked.
  - Product backlog and backlog status include BI-260014 with traceability.
- Non-goals: Multi-app deployment split, full admin feature set, and runtime plugin system in MVP.
- Constraints: Preserve graph-first workspace primacy and accepted C/D/E interaction/search patterns.
- Dependencies: Accepted A-E decisions and GraphQL contract decisions.
- Risks/assumptions:
  - Risk: module-boundary drift may increase coupling if interface discipline weakens.
  - Risk: placeholder admin routes may be misinterpreted as partially implemented admin support.
  - Assumption: single-app soft split remains sufficient through MVP validation.
- Validation approach: Route-boundary checks, architecture dependency checks, and scope-gating verification.
- Priority score: 92
- Target window: MVP foundation
- Links:
  - Requirement entry: REQ-FR-260013.md, REQ-FR-260014.md, REQ-CR-260006.md
  - ADR: ADR-260017.md
  - ICR: n/a
  - Changeset: n/a
